dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4495 IS: Reactors at Risk Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Markey (for himself and Mr. Merkley) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services

form.official-title: To require reports on the dangers posed by nuclear reactors in areas that might experience armed conflict.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Reactors at Risk Act of 2026.

section.header: Report on dangers posed by nuclear reactors in areas that might experience armed conflict

subsection.text: Not later than 120 days after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Secretary of Defense and the Under Secretary of Energy for Nuclear Security shall jointly submit to the appropriate committees of Congress a report assessing the following:

paragraph.text: The dangers posed to the national security of the United States, to the interests of allies and partners of the United States, and to the safety and security of civilian populations, by nuclear reactors in existence as of such date of enactment or scheduled to be completed during the 10-year period beginning on such date of enactment and located in the following areas:

subparagraph.text: Regions that have experienced armed conflict in the 25 years preceding such date of enactment, including the following conflicts:

clause.text: Attacks by the Russian Federation on Ukraine.
Conflict in the Middle East, including between Israel and Iran.

subparagraph.text: Areas that are contested or likely to experience armed conflict during the life span of those reactors.
Areas that would be involved in any of the following possible conflicts:

clause.text: An attack by the Russian Federation on the eastern European countries of Estonia, Latvia, Belarus, Lithuania, or Poland.
A conflict between India and Pakistan.
An attack by North Korea on South Korea.

paragraph.text: Steps the United States or allies and partners of the United States can take to prevent, prepare for, and mitigate the risks to the national security of the United States, to the interests of allies and partners of the United States, and to the safety and security of civilian populations, posed by nuclear reactors in places that may experience armed conflict.

subsection.text: The report required by subsection (a) shall be submitted in unclassified form but may include a classified annex.
In this section, the term appropriate committees of Congress means—

paragraph.text: the Committee on Armed Services, the Committee on Foreign Relations, and the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate; and
the Committee on Armed Services, the Committee on Foreign Affairs, and the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives.
